violations.text: Chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC, chicken was cooked yesterday and was observed at 47 DF. Chicken was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection and proper cooling methods were reviewed with all staff. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

ROP fish cakes/tilapia was observed thawing with ROP packaging still intact. Fish was observed at 37 DF and was taken completely out of the package at the time of inspection. Proper thawing methods reviewed with all staff. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The cutting boards in the prep unit area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the both hand-washing sinks in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes and utensils were removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was emptied and put out of service at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to use ice machine until it is properly cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Cooked shrimp, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC. shrimp was cooked yesterday and was observed at 50 DF. Shrimp was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

ROP tuna was observed thawing with ROP packaging still intact. Tuna was observed at 30DF and was completely taken out of the package at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

Knife was observed being stored in between 2 prep units. Utensils must be stored in a clean location. Knife was moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The cutting boards in the prep unit area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in coolers have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen were blocked by equipment and food products. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Bottles must remain labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Several foods in the cooling process (cooked 2 hours prior to inspection) observed placed hot in the refrigerator and on speed racks in deep buckets. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Foods were spread out on sheet pans to complete cooling properly. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Shrimp was observed thawing in a sink of stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving units in walk-in #1, as well as the outer surface of equipment (1-door glass reach-in) and gaskets, have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact areas must remain clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
